body.title: Tag test-origin inbox messages with run id; cleanup phase sweeps them
body.result: Shipped (commit 81b1c8a4): CC_INBOX_TAG env var prepends scope prefix to summary on cc_inbox.append; new delete_by_prefix() helper; conftest session fixture sets test:<ts> tag and sweeps on teardown. 9 new dedicated tests, 39 cc_inbox tests pass overall. Generalizes to debug:/sandbox:/dev: scopes.
body.description:
Tests are polluting the production cc_inbox (1510 entries triaged 2026-04-30). Stronger fix than path-isolation: self-identifying messages. Tests tag their writes with a scope+timestamp prefix, cleanup sweeps the tag. Stragglers (tests that bypass the fixture) are still findable and sweepable manually.

**Mechanism:**
- cc_inbox.append() reads `CC_INBOX_TAG` env var at append-time; when set, prepends `[<tag>]: ` to summary
- pytest conftest sets CC_INBOX_TAG=`test:<YYYYMMDD.HHMMSS.ffffff>` at session start (long timestamp aligns with other log artifacts for easy cross-linking)
- conftest.pytest_sessionfinish calls cc_inbox.delete_by_prefix(f'[{tag}]')
- New helper: cc_inbox.delete_by_prefix(prefix) for the sweep

**Generalizes beyond tests:** any scoped context (debug session, sandbox eval, dev tool) can set CC_INBOX_TAG to its own scope id (`debug:...`, `sandbox:...`) and sweep on exit. Single inbox sweep `delete_by_prefix("[test:")` nukes ALL test pollution; date-scoped `delete_by_prefix("[test:20260430")` nukes a day's runs. Same pattern works for any scope.

**Affected files:**
- lab/claudecode/cc_inbox.py (append reads env var + prefix logic; new delete_by_prefix function)
- tests/conftest.py (pytest_sessionstart sets tag; pytest_sessionfinish sweeps)

**Design rules:** test-isolation; no-real-side-effects-leaking-from-tests; side-effects-tagged-with-scope.

**Scope boundary:** IN — env-var prefix at append + delete_by_prefix helper + conftest hooks. OUT — back-tagging existing entries (already swept); changing inbox schema; non-pytest test runners; path-isolation as separate mechanism (tag-and-sweep covers the use case).

**Test plan:**
- unit: append() with CC_INBOX_TAG set prepends tag; without set, no prefix
- unit: delete_by_prefix removes only matching entries; leaves untagged + other-tagged entries intact
- integration: pytest writes a tagged entry, sessionfinish sweep removes it, the test-installed entry is gone but other entries persist (verify against tmp inbox path)
